title: Artificial Intelligence
description: Survey of artificial intelligence techniques including search algorithms, knowledge representation, planning, reasoning under uncertainty, and introduction to natural language processing and computer vision. Students study uninformed and informed search (A*, heuristic design), constraint satisfaction problems, game-playing (minimax, alpha-beta pruning), propositional and first-order logic, Bayesian networks, Markov decision processes, and reinforcement learning foundations. The course includes programming projects implementing AI agents for games, planning domains, and probabilistic reasoning. CS301 is required for algorithm design skills essential for implementing search and optimization algorithms. Note: this course is typically offered in Spring semester only.
topics: Uninformed search: BFS, DFS, iterative deepening
outcomes: Design and implement search algorithms for problem solving
Formulate problems as constraint satisfaction problems
Build game-playing agents using adversarial search
Apply probabilistic reasoning using Bayesian networks
Understand Markov Decision Processes and reinforcement learning
Evaluate AI systems in terms of rationality and performance
